commands.safety_level: High - enters configuration mode; later commands can affect production.
commands.purpose: Enters global configuration mode on a Cisco IOS device.
when_to_use: Prepare to edit interfaces, VLANs, routing, or device settings
good_output_example: No authorization or invalid input error

commands.safety_level: High - selects a production interface for possible changes.
commands.purpose: Enters configuration mode for a specific Cisco interface.
when_to_use: Apply description, VLAN, shutdown, or speed/duplex settings

commands.safety_level: Critical - can disconnect users, APs, cameras, uplinks, or services.
commands.purpose: Administratively disables the selected interface.
when_to_use: Disable an approved unused or faulty port
Stop traffic during an approved change
bad_output_example: Critical uplink or AP port shut

commands.safety_level: High - can restore traffic or introduce an unexpected device to the network.
commands.purpose: Administratively enables the selected interface.
when_to_use: Bring an approved port back into service
Recover a port that was intentionally disabled
good_output_example: Interface is no longer administratively down
Link comes up only when expected

commands.safety_level: High - changes endpoint network placement and can break access.
commands.purpose: Assigns the selected access port to a specific VLAN.
when_to_use: Move an approved endpoint to the correct VLAN
Fix an access port with the wrong VLAN
good_output_example: Endpoint gets expected network after reconnect or DHCP renewal

commands.safety_level: Medium - persists current changes; can preserve a mistake across reboot.
commands.purpose: Saves the active running configuration to startup configuration.
when_to_use: Persist an approved change across reload

commands.safety_level: Critical - reboots the device and interrupts users.
commands.purpose: Restarts the Cisco device.
good_output_example: Reload occurs during an approved window

commands.safety_level: High - enters configuration mode; later commands can affect production.
commands.purpose: Enters Comware system configuration view.
when_to_use: Start an approved Comware configuration change
Prepare to edit interfaces, VLANs, IRF, or system settings

commands.safety_level: High - selects a production interface for possible changes.

when_to_use: Apply shutdown, VLAN, description, or link settings

commands.safety_level: Critical - can disconnect users, APs, cameras, uplinks, or stack links.
commands.purpose: Administratively disables the selected Comware interface.
when_to_use: Disable an approved unused or faulty port
Stop traffic during an approved change

commands.safety_level: High - can restore traffic or introduce an unexpected device to the network.
commands.purpose: Administratively enables the selected Comware interface.
when_to_use: Bring an approved port back into service
Recover an interface that was administratively disabled

commands.safety_level: High - changes endpoint network placement and can break access.
commands.purpose: Assigns the selected Comware access port to a VLAN.
when_to_use: Move an approved endpoint to the correct VLAN

commands.safety_level: Medium - persists current changes; can preserve mistakes across reboot.
commands.purpose: Saves the current Comware configuration so it persists after reboot.

commands.safety_level: Critical - reboots equipment and interrupts users.
commands.purpose: Restarts the Comware device or member.

commands.purpose: Administratively disables the selected ArubaOS-CX interface.

commands.safety_level: High - can restore traffic or introduce an unexpected device to the network.
commands.purpose: Administratively enables the selected ArubaOS-CX interface.
when_to_use: Bring an approved interface back into service

commands.safety_level: High - changes endpoint network placement and can break access.
commands.purpose: Sets the access VLAN for the selected ArubaOS-CX interface.

commands.safety_level: Critical - reboots the device and interrupts users.
commands.purpose: Restarts the ArubaOS-CX device.

commands.safety_level: Medium - can disconnect the endpoint until DHCP renews.
commands.purpose: Releases the DHCP lease from Windows network adapters.
when_to_use: Clear a stale DHCP lease before renew
bad_output_example: Adapter has no valid IP until renewed

commands.safety_level: Medium - can briefly interrupt endpoint connectivity.
commands.purpose: Requests a new DHCP lease for Windows network adapters.
when_to_use: Recover from DHCP or APIPA state
Refresh IP settings after VLAN or DHCP changes

commands.canonical_command: netsh interface ipv4 set address name=<name> static <ip> <mask> <gateway>
commands.syntax: netsh interface ipv4 set address name=<name> static <ip> <mask> <gateway>
commands.safety_level: High - changes endpoint addressing and can break remote access.
commands.purpose: Configures a static IPv4 address on a Windows interface.
when_to_use: Recover a device that must use fixed IP settings
good_output_example: ipconfig shows intended IP, mask, and gateway

commands.safety_level: Critical - can disconnect the endpoint or remote session.

commands.safety_level: High - can change active network path and endpoint reachability.

commands.safety_level: High - changes local network stack and usually requires reboot.

commands.safety_level: Medium - changes device identity in the configuration.
commands.purpose: Sets the Cisco device hostname in global configuration mode.

bad_output_example: Wrong device name or naming-standard conflict

commands.safety_level: Medium - can affect switching when assigned to ports.
commands.purpose: Creates or enters a VLAN configuration context.

commands.safety_level: Low - documentation change within VLAN configuration.
commands.purpose: Assigns a name to the selected Cisco VLAN.
good_output_example: Name matches the approved VLAN plan

commands.safety_level: High - applies later commands to multiple interfaces.
commands.purpose: Selects multiple Cisco interfaces for a shared configuration.
when_to_use: Apply an approved identical setting to a known port set
bad_output_example: Management, uplink, or unrelated ports included

commands.purpose: Restricts which VLANs may traverse a Cisco trunk.
